cases.title: Capacity under heavy concurrent requests never exceeds EventCapacity per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
Ten or more distinct participants with no prior registration
Registration window is active
steps: Fire parallel registrationService.register calls from all participants simultaneously
Query countSeatHolders (Registered only) for the event in the database after all commits settle
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts succeed; remaining registrations are Waitlisted; testing-plan §3 scenario row satisfied: capacity under heavy concurrent requests never exceeds configured capacity

cases.title: Concurrent POST registrations and organizer GET list confirm Registered count at capacity ceiling
preconditions: Published event with registration opened, EventCapacity 3, waitlistEnabled true
Ten or more distinct participant JWTs with no prior registration
Each POST uses a unique Idempotency-Key header
Valid OrganizerAdmin JWT scoped to the event
steps: Fire parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations from all participants
Count response bodies with state Registered versus Waitlisted
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered&page=1&pageSize=100 with organizer bearer token
cases.expected: Exactly 3 POST responses return HTTP 200 with state Registered; remaining responses are HTTP 200 Waitlisted or HTTP 422 per policy; organizer GET returns HTTP 200 with total Registered count equal to EventCapacity (3) and never greater

cases.title: Sequential registrations fill seats up to EventCapacity without overflow
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with configured EventCapacity N (e.g. N=5)
Zero existing Registered participants
Registration window is active
Each participant has no prior active registration for the event
steps: Register N distinct participants sequentially via registrationService.register
After each registration, query count of seat holders (Registered state) for the event
cases.expected: Each of the N registrations receives state Registered; after the Nth registration Registered count equals EventCapacity exactly; no registration assigns Registered when count would exceed capacity

cases.title: When event is full, new registrations do not increase Registered count beyond capacity
preconditions: Event EventCapacity N with exactly N participants in Registered state
waitlistEnabled true
Registration window active
New participant with no active registration
steps: Register one additional participant via registrationService.register
Query Registered seat-holder count for the event
cases.expected: New registration has state Waitlisted (not Registered); Registered count remains N (equals EventCapacity); AC-04 and BR-03 invariants hold

cases.title: Heavy concurrent burst (12+ attempts, capacity 3) never exceeds capacity
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
12 or more unique participantIds with no prior registration
Parallel registrationService.register calls fired simultaneously
steps: Execute Promise.allSettled on all concurrent register calls
Query countSeatHolders (Registered) for the event in the database
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts succeed; exactly (attempts - capacity) registrations are Waitlisted; zero overflow beyond EventCapacity under contention

cases.title: Flow B lifecycle maintains capacity ceiling through cancel and promotion
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: full event (EventCapacity 1), one Registered, one Waitlisted
Organizer and participant tokens available
waitlistEnabled true
steps: Execute Flow B: Registered participant POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel
Verify waitlisted participant is promoted to Registered via GET registration-status
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer at each step
cases.expected: After promotion exactly one Registered participant exists; count equals EventCapacity (1) and never exceeds it at any step in the flow

cases.title: Cancellation and FIFO promotion keep Registered count at or below capacity
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 2 with two Registered participants and one Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
One Registered participant can cancel before CancellationDeadline (BR-07)
steps: Record beforeState: Registered count = 2, Waitlisted count = 1
Cancel one Registered registration to release a seat
Allow waitlist promotion to run atomically within the cancellation transaction
Record afterState: Registered count and participant states
cases.expected: afterState Registered count remains 2 (equals EventCapacity); promoted participant moves from Waitlisted to Registered; total Registered never exceeds EventCapacity during or after the promotion

cases.title: Post-insert capacity guard rolls back transaction on CAPACITY_EXCEEDED
preconditions: Race condition or guard bypass attempt where insert would push Registered count above EventCapacity
Registration module uses transactional BEGIN/COMMIT with post-insert count check
steps: Trigger registration path that inserts Registered then detects postCount > EventCapacity
Observe error and database state after rollback
cases.expected: ApiError with code CAPACITY_EXCEEDED and HTTP 422; transaction is rolled back; no persisted Registered row causes count to exceed EventCapacity

cases.title: EventCapacity boundary of 1 allows exactly one Registered participant
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 1, RegistrationOpen, waitlistEnabled true
Three distinct participants with no prior registration
steps: Register first participant → expect Registered
Register second and third participants concurrently
Query Registered count
cases.expected: Exactly one Registered row exists at all times; second and third receive Waitlisted (or one succeeds as sole Registered under race); Registered count never exceeds 1

cases.title: Full event without waitlist rejects registration without consuming a Registered seat
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 2 with two Registered participants
waitlistEnabled false
New participant with valid JWT
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations for the new participant
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL; Registered count remains 2 (at capacity, not above); no new Registered row created

cases.title: Waitlisted participants are excluded from Registered seat-holder count
preconditions: Event at EventCapacity with multiple Waitlisted registrations
Registration window still open
steps: Query countSeatHolders (or equivalent Registered-only count)
Count total rows including Waitlisted state separately
cases.expected: Seat-holder count equals Registered rows only and equals EventCapacity; Waitlisted rows do not inflate Registered count toward or beyond capacity

cases.title: Registration assignment and capacity check execute in one atomic transaction
preconditions: Event with EventCapacity 5
lockEventRuleConfig serializes concurrent writers per event
steps: Complete a successful registration that assigns Registered
Verify BEGIN/COMMIT wraps insert, post-insert count check, audit write, and waitlist enqueue (if any)
Trigger a failed capacity guard and confirm ROLLBACK
cases.expected: No intermediate state visible where Registered count exceeds EventCapacity; failed capacity guard results in full ROLLBACK with no partial seat consumption

cases.title: Unauthenticated registration attempts do not inflate Registered count
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 5 and one existing Registered participant
No Authorization header on registration requests
steps: Fire multiple parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations without Authorization header
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer
cases.expected: All unauthenticated POST requests return HTTP 401 with standard error envelope; organizer GET shows Registered count unchanged at 1; no unauthorized registration rows affect capacity invariant

cases.title: Organizer capacity meter reflects Registered count never above configured capacity
preconditions: Organizer logged in viewing event operations dashboard for an event at or near capacity
Event has known EventCapacity N and up to N Registered participants
steps: Navigate to organizer event detail or registrations view showing capacity meter
Observe registered versus capacity display
Trigger additional participant registration (via API or second browser) up to capacity
cases.expected: UI capacity meter shows Registered count less than or equal to EventCapacity at all times; when full, display indicates no remaining seats without showing registered > capacity
